title: [lichen_l2.c:519] lichen_link_rx called with potentially NULL out buffer state
description: In peer_try_all_pubkeys, when iterating through peers and calling lichen_link_rx() at line 517, the function passes out_ipv6 and out_len. If a previous iteration wrote to out_ipv6 (partial decompression before auth failure), the buffer may contain partial/garbage data. While the function resets *out_len = saved_out_len before each attempt (line 515), it does not clear out_ipv6 itself. If lichen_link_rx() fails partway through decompression, subsequent attempts use a dirty buffer. This may or may not be a problem depending on lichen_link_rx's behavior - if it always writes from byte 0, it's fine. But if SCHC decompression appends, this could cause issues.
close_reason: Invalid: lichen_link_rx() only writes to out_ipv6 buffer AFTER signature and MIC verification succeed (Step 6). If auth fails (Step 2 or 5), the function returns early without touching out_ipv6. The buffer cannot contain partial data from failed auth attempts because decompression never runs on auth failure.
